description_long: This structure is a traditional wooden pagoda with a steeply pitched, multi-tiered thatched roof, giving it a distinctive silhouette. The roof is constructed from overlapping layers of natural materials, likely straw or reeds, and features visible wooden support beams beneath the eaves. The lower section of the building is composed of wooden walls with intricate latticework and small windows, while the upper section is more enclosed. The front side has a small porch-like extension supported by posts, suggesting an entryway. The overall appearance is rustic and weathered, with darkened wood and natural textures, evoking vernacular Southeast Asian or rural architectural influences.
description: A rustic wooden pagoda with a steep, multi-tiered thatched roof and intricate latticework on the lower walls, featuring a small porch entry.
description_view_0: Front view showing the entry porch, exposed support beams, and the layered thatched roof.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the extended eaves, detailed latticework, and the depth of the roof tiers.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a more enclosed wall with a small circular window and the continuation of the roof's layered design.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the wooden latticework and the pronounced overhang of the thatched roof.
